properties.remark: A WALL OF DUST WAS INITIALLY OBSERVED VIA SATELLITE JUST SOUTH OF STOVEPIPE WELLS WHICH MOVED SOUTH ALONG HIGHWAY 190 IN BADWATER BASIN. THE DUST STORM WAS LATER CONFIRMED FROM PHOTOS ON SOCIAL MEDIA AND FROM THE DEATH VALLEY NPS TO HAVE PRODUCED VISIBILITY OF ZERO UNTIL 1445. THE DUST STORM AND ASSOCIATED STRONG WINDS PRODUCED POWER OUTAGES ACROSS FURNACE CREEK FOR OVER 6 HOURS.

A WALL OF DUST WAS INITIALLY OBSERVED VIA SATELLITE LOCATED OVER RED LAKE AZ AT 1830Z AND PROCEEDED TO SPREAD TO THE NORTHEAST, DRIVEN BY STRONG SOUTHWESTERLY WINDS. THIS DUST STORM AND ASSOCIATED ZERO MILE VISIBILITY WAS VERIFIED BY MULTIPLE TRAINED SPOTTERS AND EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT OFFICIALS. THE DUST STORM CONTINUED FOR ANOTHER 5.5 HOURS BEFORE DISSIPATING AROUND 00Z.

*** 9 INJ *** MULTIPLE VEHICLE ACCIDENT WITH 11 VEHICLES AND 9 INJURIES OCCURRED ON US 95 AROUND MILE MARKER 50 AT 1126 AM DUE TO BROWN OUT CONDITIONS RESULTING FROM A SAND STORM. US 95 WAS CLOSED FOR APPROXIMATELY 4 HOURS DUE TO THE ACCIDENT. WINDS IN THE AREA GUSTED TO 48 MPH.
